steps.title: Use the rectangle diagonal
steps.content: A rectangle's diagonal forms a right triangle with the rectangle's two side lengths as the legs. Use the Pythagorean theorem with hypotenuse $4\sqrt{17}$ and one leg $4$.

steps.title: Apply the Pythagorean theorem to the rectangle
steps.content:
In a rectangle, the diagonal and two sides form a right triangle. Let $b$ be the longer side:
$$4^2 + b^2 = (4\sqrt{17})^2$$

steps.title: Solve for the longer side
steps.content:
Square the right side:
$$(4\sqrt{17})^2 = 16 \cdot 17 = 272$$
$$16 + b^2 = 272$$
$$b^2 = 256$$
$$b = 16$$
The longer side is 16.
